column1: #            benign_unusual files are not listed here — they are the eval-only
#            FPR probe set and must NEVER be mixed into the benign/malicious
#            See benchmark/corpus/benign_unusual/ for those files and the note
#            at the bottom of this file.
# source   : self-authored          — written for this corpus
#            scraped                — pulled from a public repo (not used yet)
# notes    : one-line description of what threat signal(s) the file exercises
#            (for malicious files) or why it is clearly benign (for benign).
# Corpus size rationale — deferred decision resolved here (tech §8):
#   10 benign  / 10 malicious for the initial model selection run is the
#   minimum viable split for a 5-fold stratified CV: at least 2 samples per
#   fold per class.  This is acknowledged as small; the expectation is that
#   the model selection pass identifies which classifiers generalise at all

column2: not that it produces production-grade precision/recall.

column1: #   A second corpus pass (targeting ~30 benign / ~30 malicious) is planned
#   before the final evaluation report to make the F1 numbers meaningful.
#   The 5 benign_unusual files give a reasonable FPR probe: any model that
#   flags > 2/5 of these is over-firing and should be penalised.
